epics.title: Repository & Platform Foundation
epics.summary: Bootstrap the pnpm monorepo, TypeScript toolchain, linting, testing framework, CI pipeline, and SQLite persistence layer.
epics.why: Every subsequent task depends on a working monorepo with build, test, and lint infrastructure. This epic establishes the development platform.
goals: Working pnpm monorepo with all workspace directories
SQLite with Drizzle ORM and migration tooling
epics.risks: Stack choices (NestJS vs Fastify, Drizzle vs Kysely) must be finalized before starting. PRD recommends NestJS + Drizzle/Kysely.
epics.completion: All workspace packages build, lint, and pass an empty test suite. CI pipeline runs green.

epics.title: Domain Model & Persistence
epics.summary: Define all core domain types, database schema, migrations, and data-access repositories for the control-plane entities.
epics.why: The data model is the foundation of the entire system. Every service depends on well-typed entities and reliable persistence.
goals: All entity tables created via migrations
Type-safe domain enums and value objects
in_scope: Database migrations for all entities in docs/prd/002-data-model.md
epics.risks: Schema must precisely match the data model spec. Changes later require migrations.
epics.completion: All migrations run cleanly. All repositories pass CRUD tests. Domain types compile.

epics.title: State Machine & Transition Engine
epics.summary: Implement the Task, Worker Lease, Review Cycle, and Merge Queue Item state machines with a centralized transition service.
epics.why: The state machine is the core correctness mechanism. All orchestration depends on validated, atomic state transitions.
goals: Task state machine with all transitions from §2.1
epics.risks: State machine must exactly match docs/prd/002-data-model.md. Transition preconditions are complex.
epics.completion: All state machine transitions are validated. Integration tests cover every valid and invalid transition path.

epics.title: Packet Schemas & Validation
epics.summary: Define Zod schemas for all packet types (TaskPacket, DevResultPacket, ReviewPacket, etc.) with cross-field validation.
epics.why: Packet schemas are the contracts between control plane and workers. They must be machine-validated before any worker integration.
goals: Zod schemas for all 8 packet types
epics.risks: Schemas must exactly match the spec. Cross-field rules are tricky to implement in Zod.
epics.completion: All packet schemas validate correctly against spec examples. Cross-field rules enforced. 100% test coverage on schemas.

epics.title: Job Queue & Scheduling
epics.summary: Build the DB-backed job queue with dependency coordination and the task scheduler that matches ready tasks to worker pools.
epics.why: The scheduler and job queue drive all automated workflow progression. Without them, nothing moves autonomously.
goals: Job group coordination for review fan-out
epics.risks: Job queue must handle concurrent claims safely. Scheduler must respect all readiness constraints.
epics.completion: Jobs can be created, claimed, completed, and failed. Scheduler selects correct tasks and matches to pools.

epics.title: Lease Management & Heartbeats
epics.summary: Implement task lease acquisition, heartbeat protocol, graceful completion, stale detection, and crash recovery.
epics.why: Leases enforce exclusive task ownership and prevent duplicate work. Heartbeats enable failure detection and recovery.
epics.risks: Race conditions between heartbeat timeout and result submission. Grace period logic is subtle.
epics.completion: Leases enforce exclusivity. Heartbeat timeouts correctly reclaim work. Grace period prevents result loss.

epics.title: Dependency & Readiness Engine
epics.summary: Build the task dependency graph manager with DAG validation, readiness computation, and automatic recalculation.
epics.why: Dependency-aware scheduling is a core product feature. Tasks must not start until prerequisites are satisfied.
goals: Automatic readiness recalculation on task completion
epics.risks: Graph operations must be efficient. Reconciliation loop must be idempotent.
epics.completion: Dependencies are validated as a DAG. Task readiness correctly reflects dependency status. Reconciliation catches missed updates.

epics.title: Workspace Management
epics.summary: Implement git worktree-based workspace provisioning, packet/config mounting, cleanup, and reconciliation.
epics.why: Each worker needs an isolated workspace. The workspace manager provides reproducible, policy-governed execution environments.
goals: Task packet and config mounting into workspace
epics.risks: Git worktree operations can fail if the repo state is unexpected. Cleanup must handle edge cases.
epics.completion: Worktrees created and cleaned up correctly. Packets mounted into workspace. Reconciliation removes expired workspaces.

epics.title: Worker Runtime & Execution
epics.summary: Define the worker runtime interface, build the Worker Supervisor, implement the Copilot CLI adapter, and the policy-aware command wrapper.
epics.why: Workers execute the actual AI agent work. The runtime abstraction enables pluggable execution backends while enforcing policies.
goals: Structured output capture and schema validation
epics.risks: Copilot CLI integration details may require experimentation. Command wrapper must be comprehensive.
epics.completion: Worker Supervisor can spawn, monitor, and teardown worker processes. Copilot CLI adapter executes tasks and captures structured output.

epics.title: Policy & Configuration
epics.summary: Implement the hierarchical configuration system, command/file/validation/retry/escalation policies, and effective policy snapshot generation.
epics.why: Policies govern all worker behavior and system safety. The configuration system must resolve hierarchical overrides deterministically.
epics.risks: Policy resolution precedence must exactly match the spec. Edge cases in file scope overlap require careful testing.
epics.completion: All policy types enforce correctly. Config resolution follows precedence order. Snapshots are persisted per run.

epics.title: Validation Runner
epics.summary: Build the validation runner that executes test/lint/build checks, emits ValidationResultPackets, and enforces validation gates.
epics.why: Validation gates enforce quality at every stage transition. No task can progress without passing required checks.
epics.risks: Validation commands may fail for environment-specific reasons. Must handle timeouts and partial results.
epics.completion: Validation runner executes checks per profile. Results emitted as valid packets. Gates block invalid transitions.

epics.title: Review Pipeline
epics.summary: Implement the review router, specialist reviewer dispatch, lead reviewer consolidation, decision application, and rework loop.
epics.why: The review pipeline provides quality assurance for all autonomous work. It prevents bad code from reaching the merge queue.
goals: Specialist reviewer dispatch via job queue
Lead reviewer consolidation with job dependencies
epics.risks: Review fan-out coordination is complex. Must handle partial reviewer failures.
epics.completion: Reviews route correctly by file/tag/risk. Lead reviewer consolidates and emits decisions. Rework loop feeds rejection context.

epics.title: Merge Pipeline
epics.summary: Build the merge queue, merge execution (rebase/squash/merge-commit), conflict classification, post-merge validation, and failure handling.
epics.why: The merge pipeline is the final integration step. It must be reliable, serialized, and recover gracefully from failures.
epics.risks: Git merge operations are inherently complex. Post-merge validation failures require careful handling.
epics.completion: Merge queue processes items in correct order. Merges execute with chosen strategy. Conflicts classified correctly. Post-merge failures handled per policy.

epics.title: Artifact Service
epics.summary: Implement filesystem-based artifact storage, reference resolution, summarization packet generation, and partial work snapshots.
epics.why: Artifacts are the audit trail and context transfer mechanism. Every packet, log, and output must be persisted and retrievable.
epics.risks: Filesystem operations are not transactional. Must handle concurrent writes carefully.
epics.completion: Artifacts stored in correct directory structure. References resolve correctly. Summaries generated for retries.

epics.title: Audit & Event System
epics.summary: Implement audit event recording on every state transition, audit query service, and structured logging with correlation.
epics.why: Complete audit trail is a product requirement. Operators must be able to reconstruct what happened for any task.
goals: Audit event on every state transition
epics.risks: Audit events must be written atomically with state transitions. Logging must not impact performance.
epics.completion: Every state transition produces an audit event. Events queryable by entity and time range. Logs are structured and correlated.

epics.title: Observability
epics.summary: Integrate OpenTelemetry tracing, implement Prometheus metrics endpoint, and instrument core orchestration paths.
epics.why: Operators need to trace task execution across services, monitor system health, and detect problems proactively.
epics.risks: Instrumentation adds overhead. Must keep cardinality low per label rules.
epics.completion: Traces cover task lifecycle. Metrics endpoint returns expected counters/histograms/gauges.

epics.title: REST API Layer
epics.summary: Build the NestJS control plane API with CRUD endpoints for all entities and management operations.
epics.why: The API is the interface between the UI, operators, and the control plane. All external interaction goes through it.
goals: NestJS app bootstrap with module structure
epics.risks: API design must be stable early since UI depends on it. Versioning strategy needed.
epics.completion: All entity CRUD operations available via REST. Endpoints validated and documented.

epics.title: Real-time Events
epics.summary: Implement WebSocket/SSE gateway for live task state updates, worker heartbeats, and queue status broadcasting.
epics.why: The UI needs real-time updates to show live system state without polling.
epics.risks: WebSocket connections need lifecycle management. Must handle disconnects gracefully.
epics.completion: Clients receive real-time updates for state changes, heartbeats, and queue movements.

epics.title: Web UI Foundation
epics.summary: Bootstrap the React SPA with Vite, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, TanStack Query, WebSocket client, and app shell.
epics.why: The UI is the primary operator interface. The foundation must be solid before building feature views.
epics.risks: UI framework choices are hard to change later. Component library selection matters.
epics.completion: SPA loads with navigation shell. API calls work. WebSocket connects and receives events.

epics.title: Web UI Feature Views
epics.summary: Build all primary UI views: dashboard, task board, task detail, worker pools, reviews, merge queue, config editor, audit explorer.
epics.why: These views provide the operational visibility that is a core product goal.
epics.risks: UI complexity is high. Must prioritize views that enable operational oversight.
epics.completion: All primary views render correctly with real and empty data states.

epics.title: Operator Actions & Overrides
epics.summary: Implement the operator action API, state transition guards for manual actions, escalation resolution, and UI controls.
epics.why: Operators need to intervene in automated workflows. This epic provides safe, audited manual override capabilities.
goals: UI controls in task detail, pool, and merge views
epics.risks: Manual overrides must respect state machine invariants. Must prevent unsafe transitions.
epics.completion: All operator actions available via API and UI. Actions audited. Guards prevent invalid transitions.

epics.title: Integration Testing & E2E
epics.summary: Build the integration test harness and comprehensive end-to-end tests for all critical paths.
epics.why: End-to-end tests validate that all components work together correctly. Critical for confidence in autonomous operation.
goals: Test harness with fake runner and workspace
epics.risks: Integration tests are brittle if not designed carefully. Must use proper test doubles.
epics.completion: All critical paths tested end-to-end. Tests pass reliably. Milestone acceptance criteria verified.

tasks.title: Initialize pnpm monorepo workspace
tasks.desc: Create the root pnpm monorepo with workspace definitions for apps/control-plane, apps/web-ui, apps/worker-runner, and shared packages (domain, application, infrastructure, schemas, config, observability, ui-components, testing). Establishes the project layout described in docs/prd/007-technical-architecture.md §7.4.
tasks.goal: Establish the foundational monorepo structure so all subsequent packages can be developed in parallel.
in_scope: Root package.json with private:true and pnpm workspace config
Directory scaffolding for all apps and packages
Minimal package.json for each workspace with @factory/ scope
criteria: pnpm install succeeds with no errors
pnpm ls --recursive shows all workspace packages
All package.json files have @factory/ scoped names
tasks.validation: Run pnpm install and verify workspace linking
tasks.risks: None significant. This is purely scaffolding.

tasks.title: Configure TypeScript for all packages
tasks.desc: Set up TypeScript configuration with a root tsconfig.json base and per-package tsconfig.json files. Configure path aliases, strict mode, and project references for incremental builds.
tasks.goal: Enable TypeScript compilation across all workspace packages with consistent settings.
criteria: pnpm --recursive run build succeeds (even if output is empty)
Path aliases resolve correctly across packages
tasks.validation: Run pnpm -r build and verify no TypeScript errors
tasks.risks: Path alias configuration can be tricky in monorepos. Test cross-package imports.

tasks.title: Set up ESLint and Prettier
tasks.desc: Configure ESLint with TypeScript support and Prettier for consistent code formatting across the monorepo.
tasks.goal: Enforce consistent code style and catch common errors across all packages.
in_scope: lint and format scripts in root package.json
criteria: pnpm lint runs without errors on existing code
tasks.validation: Run pnpm lint && pnpm format:check
tasks.risks: ESLint flat config is relatively new. Ensure compatibility with TypeScript plugin.

tasks.title: Set up Vitest testing framework
tasks.desc: Configure Vitest as the test runner for the monorepo with workspace support, coverage reporting, and test path aliases.
tasks.goal: Provide a fast, TypeScript-native test framework for all packages.
in_scope: Test scripts in root and per-package
criteria: pnpm test runs successfully (even with 0 tests initially)
tasks.validation: Run pnpm test and verify framework initializes
tasks.risks: Vitest workspace configuration must align with pnpm workspaces.

tasks.title: Create CI pipeline with GitHub Actions
tasks.desc: Create a GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs lint, typecheck, and tests on every push and pull request.
tasks.goal: Catch errors early and maintain code quality through automated CI.
criteria: CI workflow file exists and is valid YAML
Workflow runs lint, typecheck, and test steps
pnpm caching is configured for performance
tasks.validation: Push a commit and verify CI runs all checks
tasks.risks: CI environment may differ from local. Ensure Node.js version matches .nvmrc.

tasks.title: Set up SQLite with Drizzle ORM and migrations
tasks.desc: Configure SQLite with WAL mode and Drizzle ORM in the control-plane app. Set up the migration system and database connection management.
tasks.goal: Establish the persistence foundation so domain entities can be stored and queried.
in_scope: Database connection factory with WAL and BEGIN IMMEDIATE
criteria: SQLite database file is created with WAL mode
Connection uses BEGIN IMMEDIATE for write transactions
tasks.validation: Run db:migrate and verify database is created with WAL mode
tasks.risks: better-sqlite3 requires native compilation. Ensure build tools are available.

tasks.title: Define core domain enums and value objects
tasks.desc: Create TypeScript enums and types for all domain concepts: task states, worker lease states, review cycle states, merge queue item states, job types/statuses, dependency types, validation run scopes, and all other enumerated values from the data model.
tasks.goal: Establish the type-safe vocabulary for the entire domain.
criteria: All domain enums from docs/prd/002-data-model.md are defined
TypeScript compilation succeeds with strict mode
Enum values exactly match the spec (case-sensitive)
tasks.validation: pnpm -r build && pnpm test --filter @factory/domain
tasks.risks: Must keep enums precisely aligned with docs/prd/002-data-model.md.

tasks.title: Create migrations for Project, Repository, WorkflowTemplate tables
tasks.desc: Create the Drizzle schema definitions and migration for Project, Repository, and WorkflowTemplate tables as specified in docs/prd/002-data-model.md §2.3.
tasks.goal: Persist project and repository metadata so tasks can be scoped to repositories.
in_scope: Project table with all fields from §2.3
criteria: Migration creates all three tables with correct columns and types
Foreign key from Repository to Project is enforced
tasks.validation: Run migration and query sqlite_master to verify tables
tasks.risks: JSON column types in SQLite are text. Ensure Drizzle handles serialization.

tasks.title: Create migrations for Task and TaskDependency tables
tasks.desc: Create Drizzle schema and migration for the Task and TaskDependency tables with all fields from docs/prd/002-data-model.md §2.3, including the version column for optimistic concurrency.
tasks.goal: Enable task persistence with full lifecycle metadata and dependency tracking.
in_scope: Task table with ALL fields from §2.3 including version, retry_count, review_round_count
TaskDependency table with dependency_type and is_hard_block
criteria: Task table has all fields from docs/prd/002-data-model.md
TaskDependency table supports all dependency types
tasks.validation: Run migration and verify table schemas match spec
tasks.risks: Task table has many columns. Ensure all are included per spec.

tasks.title: Create migrations for WorkerPool, Worker, AgentProfile, PromptTemplate tables
tasks.desc: Create Drizzle schema and migration for WorkerPool, Worker, AgentProfile, and PromptTemplate tables from docs/prd/002-data-model.md §2.3.
tasks.goal: Enable worker pool management and agent profile configuration persistence.
in_scope: WorkerPool with all fields including capabilities as JSON
Worker with status and health metadata
AgentProfile with all policy reference IDs
PromptTemplate with template_text and schemas as JSON
Foreign keys between Worker->WorkerPool and AgentProfile->WorkerPool
criteria: All four tables created with correct schemas
tasks.validation: Run migration and test insert/select for each table
tasks.risks: Many JSON columns — ensure consistent serialization approach.

tasks.title: Create migrations for TaskLease, ReviewCycle, ReviewPacket, LeadReviewDecision tables
tasks.desc: Create Drizzle schema and migration for TaskLease, ReviewCycle, ReviewPacket, and LeadReviewDecision tables.
tasks.goal: Enable lease tracking, review cycle management, and review decision persistence.
in_scope: ReviewCycle with required/optional reviewers as JSON
criteria: All four tables created with correct schemas
Foreign keys reference Task table correctly
JSON columns handle array and object types
tasks.validation: Run migration and verify table schemas
tasks.risks: Review tables have complex relationships. Ensure FK integrity.

tasks.title: Create migrations for MergeQueueItem, ValidationRun, Job tables
tasks.desc: Create Drizzle schema and migration for MergeQueueItem, ValidationRun, and Job tables.
tasks.goal: Enable merge queue tracking, validation result storage, and job queue persistence.
in_scope: ValidationRun with run_scope and artifact_refs as JSON
Job with all fields including parent_job_id, job_group_id, depends_on_job_ids as JSON
Indexes on Job.status and Job.run_after for queue polling
criteria: All three tables created with correct schemas
Job table supports dependency and group coordination fields
tasks.validation: Run migration and verify table schemas and indexes
tasks.risks: Job table is heavily used. Index design matters for performance.

tasks.title: Create migrations for AuditEvent and PolicySet tables
tasks.desc: Create Drizzle schema and migration for AuditEvent and PolicySet tables.
tasks.goal: Enable audit trail persistence and policy set storage.
in_scope: AuditEvent with all fields from §2.3 including metadata_json
PolicySet with all policy JSON columns
criteria: Both tables created with correct schemas
AuditEvent indexes support entity and time range queries
PolicySet JSON columns handle complex policy objects
tasks.validation: Run migration and verify table schemas
tasks.risks: AuditEvent table will grow large. Consider retention policies early.

tasks.title: Implement data access repositories for all entities
tasks.desc: Implement the data access (repository) layer for all entities using Drizzle ORM. Each repository provides typed CRUD operations, query methods, and transaction support. The Task repository must include optimistic concurrency via the version column.
tasks.goal: Provide a clean, tested data access layer that all application services build upon.
in_scope: Repository classes for: Project, Repository, Task (with version check), TaskDependency, WorkerPool, Worker, AgentProfile, PromptTemplate, TaskLease, ReviewCycle, ReviewPacket, LeadReviewDecision, MergeQueueItem, ValidationRun, Job, AuditEvent, PolicySet
criteria: Repository exists for every entity in the data model
Job repository supports atomic claim operation
All repositories have passing unit tests with in-memory SQLite
tasks.validation: Run unit tests for all repositories
tasks.risks: In-memory SQLite may behave slightly differently from file-based. Test both if possible.

tasks.title: Implement Task state machine with transition validation
tasks.desc: Implement the Task state machine as a pure domain module. Define all valid transitions, preconditions for each transition, and transition validation logic. This is the core correctness mechanism of the system.
tasks.goal: Ensure only valid state transitions can occur, matching the spec in docs/prd/002-data-model.md §2.1 exactly.
in_scope: Validation that current state allows target state
criteria: All valid transitions from §2.1 are allowed
All invalid transitions are rejected with descriptive reasons
Guard functions check preconditions (e.g., READY->ASSIGNED requires lease)
tasks.validation: Run domain package tests
tasks.risks: Transition rules are complex. Must exactly match the spec.

tasks.title: Implement supporting state machines
tasks.desc: Implement state machines for Worker Lease (§2.2), Review Cycle (§2.2), and Merge Queue Item (§2.2) with transition validation.
tasks.goal: Provide validated state management for all supporting lifecycle entities.
in_scope: Worker Lease states: IDLE through RECLAIMED
Review Cycle states: NOT_STARTED through ESCALATED
Merge Queue Item states: ENQUEUED through FAILED
out_scope: Integration with main Task state machine
criteria: All three state machines implemented with correct transitions
tasks.validation: pnpm test --filter @factory/domain
tasks.risks: Supporting state machines are simpler but must stay consistent with the main task state machine.

tasks.title: Build centralized State Transition Service
tasks.desc: Create the centralized State Transition Service that is the single authority for committing state changes. All modules must call this service rather than updating state directly.
tasks.goal: Centralize state mutation to enforce invariants, emit events, and maintain audit trail atomically.
in_scope: TransitionService with transitionTask(), transitionLease(), transitionReviewCycle(), transitionMergeQueueItem()
Precondition validation via state machine modules
Audit event emission within same transaction
out_scope: Specific transition triggers (those belong in application services)
criteria: All state transitions go through this service
Audit event created atomically with state change
tasks.validation: Integration tests with real SQLite database
tasks.risks: Transaction boundaries must be correct. Events must not fire on rollback.

tasks.title: Implement atomic transition + audit persistence
tasks.desc: Ensure that state transitions and their corresponding audit events are persisted atomically in a single database transaction using BEGIN IMMEDIATE.
tasks.goal: Guarantee that no state change can occur without an audit record, and no audit record exists without a committed state change.
in_scope: Rollback on audit event write failure
criteria: State change and audit event are in the same transaction
Failed transitions leave no partial state
Concurrent conflicting transitions are safely rejected
tasks.validation: Integration test with concurrent transition attempts
tasks.risks: SQLite concurrency is limited. BEGIN IMMEDIATE helps but must be tested.

tasks.title: Implement optimistic concurrency control
tasks.desc: Implement and thoroughly test the optimistic concurrency control mechanism on Task entities. Every state transition must check Task.version matches expected value and increment it atomically.
tasks.goal: Prevent race conditions where two actors attempt to transition the same task simultaneously.
in_scope: Version check in UPDATE WHERE clause
criteria: Concurrent transitions to the same task are safely handled
Only one transition succeeds when two race
Conflict produces a clear, typed error
tasks.validation: Concurrent update tests
tasks.risks: Testing concurrency in SQLite requires careful setup. Use real file-based DB for these tests.

tasks.title: Define shared Zod types for packets
tasks.desc: Create the shared Zod type definitions used across all packet schemas: FileChangeSummary, Issue, ValidationCheckResult, and common enums.
tasks.goal: Establish reusable schema building blocks for all packet types.
in_scope: Common enums (status, severity, verdict, decision, confidence)
criteria: All three shared types defined with Zod
Invalid data is rejected with clear errors
tasks.validation: Schema validation tests
tasks.risks: Zod version matters — use v3.x for stability.

tasks.title: Define TaskPacket and DevResultPacket Zod schemas
tasks.desc: Create Zod schemas for TaskPacket (§8.4) and DevResultPacket (§8.5) with all required fields and nested structures.
tasks.goal: Machine-validate the primary input and output contracts for developer workers.
in_scope: TaskPacket with all required top-level fields from §8.4.3
DevResultPacket with all required fields from §8.5.3
Nested structures (task, repository, workspace, context, policies, validation_requirements)
tasks.validation: Schema validation tests with spec examples
tasks.risks: Spec examples are extensive. Must match exactly.

tasks.title: Define ReviewPacket and LeadReviewDecisionPacket schemas
tasks.desc: Create Zod schemas for ReviewPacket (§8.6) and LeadReviewDecisionPacket (§8.7).
tasks.goal: Machine-validate review and lead review decision outputs.
in_scope: ReviewPacket with verdict, blocking/non-blocking issues, confidence
LeadReviewDecisionPacket with decision, deduplication notes, follow-up refs
tasks.validation: pnpm test --filter @factory/schemas
tasks.risks: None significant.

tasks.title: Define remaining packet schemas
tasks.desc: Create Zod schemas for MergePacket (§8.8), MergeAssistPacket (§8.9), ValidationResultPacket (§8.10), PostMergeAnalysisPacket (§8.11), and the PolicySnapshot schema.
tasks.goal: Complete the packet schema library covering all artifact types.
in_scope: ValidationResultPacket with run_scope and checks array
criteria: All five schemas defined and tested
tasks.validation: pnpm test --filter @factory/schemas
tasks.risks: PolicySnapshot is complex with many nested policy objects.

tasks.title: Implement cross-field validation and schema versioning
tasks.desc: Add cross-field validation rules from §8.13 to all packet schemas and implement schema version validation with multi-version support from §8.15.
tasks.goal: Ensure packet schemas enforce business rules that span multiple fields and support version evolution.
in_scope: Cross-field rules: ReviewPacket blocking_issues empty when approved, LeadReviewDecision changes_requested requires blocking issues, etc.
criteria: All cross-field rules from §8.13 are enforced
Invalid cross-field combinations rejected with descriptive errors
Schema version validation accepts same-major, rejects cross-major
tasks.validation: pnpm test --filter @factory/schemas -- --grep cross-field
tasks.risks: Zod .refine() error messages must be clear for debugging schema validation failures.

tasks.title: Implement DB-backed job queue
tasks.desc: Implement the core DB-backed job queue with create, claim, complete, and fail operations using the Job table. Claims must be atomic and prevent duplicate processing.
tasks.goal: Provide a reliable job processing mechanism backed by SQLite.
criteria: Jobs can be created and claimed atomically
No two workers can claim the same job
run_after is respected (future jobs not claimed early)
Complete and fail transitions work correctly
tasks.validation: Concurrent claim tests with multiple simulated workers
tasks.risks: SQLite concurrent writes are serialized. This is fine for V1 but limits throughput.

tasks.title: Implement job dependency and group coordination
tasks.desc: Extend the job queue to support depends_on_job_ids and job_group_id coordination. A job with dependencies cannot be claimed until all dependency jobs reach terminal status.
tasks.goal: Enable review fan-out where specialist reviewer jobs run in parallel and the lead reviewer job waits for all to complete.
in_scope: Job group queries (find all jobs in group)
Terminal status detection for dependency jobs (completed or failed)
Automatic dependency resolution on job completion
criteria: Jobs with unmet dependencies cannot be claimed
Jobs become claimable when all dependencies reach terminal status
tasks.validation: Integration test with review fan-out scenario
tasks.risks: Dependency check adds a query per claim. Acceptable for V1.

tasks.title: Implement Scheduler service
tasks.desc: Build the Scheduler that selects the next ready task, matches it to a compatible worker pool, and triggers lease acquisition.
tasks.goal: Automate task-to-worker assignment based on readiness, priority, and pool compatibility.
in_scope: Query ready tasks ordered by priority
Pool compatibility matching (capabilities, repo scope, pool type)
criteria: Pool matching respects capabilities and concurrency limits
Lease acquired atomically with state transition
tasks.validation: Integration test with multiple tasks and pools
tasks.risks: Pool matching logic must be extensible. Start simple, evolve.

tasks.title: Implement scheduler tick loop
tasks.desc: Create the scheduler tick as a recurring background job that periodically invokes the Scheduler service to process ready tasks.
tasks.goal: Make scheduling automatic rather than requiring manual triggers.
in_scope: Scheduler tick job creation on application start
criteria: Scheduler runs periodically without manual intervention
Scheduler stops trying when no tasks or pools are available
tasks.validation: Start app and observe scheduler processing tasks
tasks.risks: Tick must be idempotent. Multiple ticks running concurrently must not cause issues.

tasks.title: Implement reconciliation sweep job
tasks.desc: Create the reconciliation sweep as a recurring background job that detects and fixes inconsistent state: stale leases, orphaned jobs, tasks stuck in transitional states, and dependency recalculation.
tasks.goal: Ensure the system self-heals from transient failures and missed events.
in_scope: Tasks stuck in ASSIGNED without heartbeat
criteria: Stale leases are detected and reclaimed
tasks.validation: Create intentionally stale leases and verify reconciliation
tasks.risks: Reconciliation must not interfere with in-progress work. Check carefully.

tasks.title: Implement lease acquisition with exclusivity
tasks.desc: Implement lease acquisition that enforces the one-active-lease-per-task invariant. Use atomic DB operations to prevent duplicate leases.
tasks.goal: Guarantee that exactly one worker can be assigned to a task at any time.
in_scope: Check no active lease exists for the task
Reject if task not in READY state
criteria: Only one active lease per task at any time
Concurrent acquisition attempts: exactly one succeeds
Lease has correct TTL and expiry
tasks.validation: Concurrent lease acquisition tests
tasks.risks: Critical invariant. Must be thoroughly tested with concurrent scenarios.

tasks.title: Implement heartbeat receive and staleness detection
tasks.desc: Implement heartbeat reception from workers and staleness detection based on missed heartbeat thresholds.
tasks.goal: Enable the control plane to detect unresponsive workers and trigger recovery.
criteria: Stale leases detected after missed threshold + grace
TTL expiry detected regardless of heartbeat status
Invalid heartbeats (wrong lease, completed lease) are rejected
tasks.validation: Test staleness detection with time manipulation
tasks.risks: Time-based tests can be flaky. Use a fake clock for deterministic testing.

tasks.title: Implement graceful completion protocol
tasks.desc: Implement the terminal heartbeat protocol: workers send completing:true before emitting results, and the lease manager extends the grace window.
tasks.goal: Prevent race conditions where a result is submitted just as a lease is being reclaimed.
in_scope: Grace period extension on terminal heartbeat
Result acceptance within grace period after stale marking
criteria: Terminal heartbeat transitions lease to COMPLETING
Grace period extended after terminal heartbeat
Results within grace period are accepted even after stale marking
Late results past grace period are rejected
tasks.validation: Race condition test with terminal heartbeat and result submission
tasks.risks: Grace period logic is the most subtle part of the lease protocol. Test thoroughly.

tasks.title: Implement stale lease reclaim and retry/escalation
tasks.desc: Implement the lease reclaim flow: when a lease is detected as stale, reclaim the workspace, snapshot partial work, and apply retry or escalation policy.
tasks.goal: Recover from worker failures while preserving partial work for context.
in_scope: Task transition based on retry eligibility (FAILED or back to READY)
criteria: Stale leases are reclaimed with correct terminal state
Retry-exhausted tasks move to FAILED or ESCALATED per policy
tasks.validation: Test reclaim with various retry/escalation combinations
tasks.risks: Must handle the edge case where a result arrives during reclaim processing.

tasks.title: Implement crash recovery with partial artifact capture
tasks.desc: When a lease is reclaimed, capture partial work artifacts from the workspace for context in retry attempts.
tasks.goal: Preserve partial work so retry attempts have context about what was already done.
in_scope: Store snapshot as partial_result_artifact_refs in lease record
Include partial artifacts in next TaskPacket via context.prior_partial_work
Network partition handling: check workspace for filesystem-persisted result packet
criteria: Partial artifacts captured on reclaim when no valid result found
Filesystem-persisted results are checked before reclaim
Prior partial work included in retry task packets
tasks.validation: Test crash recovery with and without filesystem-persisted results
tasks.risks: Filesystem state may be inconsistent after a crash. Capture what's available, don't fail on errors.

tasks.title: Implement DAG validation with circular dependency detection
tasks.desc: Implement circular dependency detection when adding task dependencies. The dependency graph must be validated as a DAG on every insert.
tasks.goal: Prevent circular dependencies that would make tasks permanently blocked.
in_scope: addDependency(taskId, dependsOnTaskId, type, isHardBlock) with cycle check
Graph traversal to detect cycles before insertion
Rejection with descriptive error on cycle detection
Support for all dependency types: blocks, relates_to, parent_child
criteria: Circular dependencies are detected and rejected
tasks.validation: Unit tests with various graph topologies
tasks.risks: DFS on large graphs could be slow. Acceptable for V1 task counts.

tasks.title: Implement readiness computation
tasks.desc: Implement the readiness computation that determines whether a task should be READY or BLOCKED based on its hard-block dependencies.
tasks.goal: Ensure tasks only become READY when all hard-block prerequisites are DONE.
in_scope: Hard-block: task can't be READY until dependency reaches DONE
Soft-block (is_hard_block=false): informational only, doesn't affect readiness
parent_child: parent blocks until all children DONE/CANCELLED
criteria: Tasks with unsatisfied hard-block deps are BLOCKED
Tasks with all hard-block deps DONE are READY
tasks.validation: Tests with various dependency configurations
tasks.risks: Must handle FAILED and CANCELLED dependency states correctly per spec.

tasks.title: Implement reverse-dependency recalculation
tasks.desc: When a task transitions to DONE, automatically recalculate readiness for all reverse-dependent tasks and transition them from BLOCKED to READY as appropriate.
tasks.goal: Automatically unblock downstream tasks when their prerequisites complete.
in_scope: On task DONE: find all tasks that depend on this task
Transition BLOCKED->READY for newly eligible tasks
Handle FAILED/CANCELLED: hard-blocked dependents remain BLOCKED
out_scope: Notification to operators about newly ready tasks
criteria: Completing a task unblocks ready dependents automatically
Multiple dependencies correctly evaluated (all must be satisfied)
tasks.validation: Integration test with dependency chains
tasks.risks: Must be idempotent — safe to recalculate multiple times.

tasks.title: Implement dependency reconciliation loop
tasks.desc: Add dependency readiness recalculation to the reconciliation sweep to catch any missed recalculations.
tasks.goal: Ensure no task stays incorrectly BLOCKED due to a missed event.
in_scope: Periodic scan of all BLOCKED tasks
Transition BLOCKED->READY if dependencies now satisfied
out_scope: Performance optimization for large task sets
criteria: Tasks are transitioned to READY when eligible
Reconciliation is idempotent and safe to run frequently
tasks.validation: Create a BLOCKED task with satisfied deps, verify reconciliation unblocks it
tasks.risks: Full scan of BLOCKED tasks could be slow with many tasks. Acceptable for V1.

tasks.title: Implement git worktree creation per task
tasks.desc: Implement workspace provisioning using git worktrees. Each task gets an isolated worktree with a dedicated branch following the factory/{task_id} naming convention.
tasks.goal: Provide isolated workspaces so workers cannot interfere with each other.
in_scope: Git worktree add with branch creation
Workspace reuse for retries if available
tasks.validation: Create and verify worktrees against a test git repo
tasks.risks: Git worktree requires the main repo to be checked out. Test with a real git repo.

tasks.title: Implement workspace packet and config mounting
tasks.desc: Mount the task packet JSON, run config, and effective policy snapshot into the workspace directory before worker execution.
tasks.goal: Ensure the worker has all necessary context files when it starts.
in_scope: Validate all files are valid JSON before mounting
criteria: Task packet, run config, and policy snapshot written to workspace
tasks.validation: Mount packets and verify file contents
tasks.risks: File write permissions could be an issue. Handle gracefully.

tasks.title: Implement workspace cleanup for terminal states
tasks.desc: Implement workspace cleanup that removes worktrees and branches for tasks in terminal states (DONE, FAILED, CANCELLED) after the retention period.
tasks.goal: Prevent disk space exhaustion from accumulated workspaces.
in_scope: Retain FAILED workspaces per retain_failed_workspaces policy
criteria: Workspaces cleaned up after retention period
tasks.validation: Test cleanup with various task states and retention periods
tasks.risks: Branch deletion must be careful — only delete branches for terminal tasks.

tasks.title: Implement ReconcileWorkspacesCommand
tasks.desc: Create a scheduled job that periodically scans for expired workspaces and cleans them up.
tasks.goal: Automate workspace cleanup so operators don't need to manage disk space manually.
in_scope: Scan for workspaces past retention period
criteria: Cleanup runs periodically without manual intervention
tasks.validation: Create expired workspaces and verify cleanup job removes them
tasks.risks: Must not clean up workspaces for active tasks. Double-check task state.

tasks.title: Define worker runtime interface
tasks.desc: Define the TypeScript interface for worker runtime adapters: prepareRun, startRun, streamRun, cancelRun, collectArtifacts, finalizeRun.
tasks.goal: Establish a stable, pluggable contract so execution backends can be swapped without changing orchestration.
in_scope: WorkerRuntime interface with all methods from §10.8.2
RunContext type (task packet, policy snapshot, workspace paths, timeout settings)
criteria: Interface defined with all required methods
RunContext and RunResult types are comprehensive
tasks.validation: TypeScript compilation succeeds
tasks.risks: Interface must be stable — changes affect all adapters.

tasks.title: Implement Worker Supervisor
tasks.desc: Build the Worker Supervisor that spawns worker processes, monitors their lifecycle, tracks heartbeats, mediates workspace access, and handles process termination.
tasks.goal: Manage the full lifecycle of worker processes from spawn to cleanup.
in_scope: spawnWorker(runContext) — create workspace, mount packets, start adapter
Worker entity creation and status updates
criteria: Workers spawn correctly with workspace and packets
tasks.validation: Integration test with a mock adapter
tasks.risks: Process management is OS-dependent. Test on target platform.

tasks.title: Implement Copilot CLI execution adapter
tasks.desc: Implement the Copilot CLI adapter that translates the worker runtime interface into Copilot CLI invocations with proper prompt injection, output capture, and policy enforcement.
tasks.goal: Enable AI worker execution via GitHub Copilot CLI as the primary V1 execution backend.
in_scope: Implement WorkerRuntime interface for Copilot CLI
Prompt injection with role and task context
Schema validation of final output packet
criteria: Copilot CLI process spawned with correct arguments
Prompt includes role, task context, and output schema expectations
Logs captured separately from structured output
tasks.validation: End-to-end test with Copilot CLI on a sample repo
tasks.risks: Copilot CLI interface details may require experimentation. Create a spike first if needed.

tasks.title: Implement structured output capture and validation
tasks.desc: Implement the result packet extraction, schema validation, and acceptance logic that runs after a worker completes. Enforce the rule that no result is accepted without valid schema.
tasks.goal: Ensure all worker outputs meet the schema contract before being accepted into the system.
in_scope: Extract structured packet from worker output (file or delimiter-based)
Verify all IDs match orchestrator context
Schema repair attempt for minor violations
Rejection and failure handling for invalid output
criteria: Schema violations handled with repair attempt
tasks.validation: Tests with valid, invalid, and repairable packet outputs
tasks.risks: Schema repair logic must be conservative — don't accept clearly wrong data.

tasks.title: Implement policy-aware command wrapper
tasks.desc: Build the command execution wrapper that enforces command policy (allowlist, denied patterns, forbidden args) before executing any shell command on behalf of a worker.
tasks.goal: Prevent workers from executing unauthorized or dangerous commands.
in_scope: Structured command arguments (not raw shell strings)
Shell compound command and subshell blocking
Policy violation artifact emission on denied command
criteria: Denied commands are blocked with clear error
Forbidden arg patterns caught after allowlist pass
Shell compound commands blocked when policy says no
tasks.validation: Tests with allowlist and various violation scenarios
tasks.risks: Pattern matching must be thorough. Attackers may try to bypass allowlists.

tasks.title: Implement command policy model and enforcement
tasks.desc: Define the command policy data model and enforcement logic from docs/prd/009-policy-and-enforcement-spec.md §9.3.
tasks.goal: Establish command execution governance that workers must respect.
in_scope: CommandPolicy type with mode, allowed_commands, denied_patterns, forbidden_arg_patterns
Policy resolution for a given run
Default V1 command policy with reasonable allowlist
out_scope: Command wrapper implementation (T047, depends on this)
criteria: Default policy provides reasonable V1 allowlist
Policy can be loaded and resolved
tasks.validation: Unit tests for policy type and resolution
tasks.risks: Default allowlist must be carefully curated — too restrictive blocks work, too permissive is unsafe.

tasks.title: Implement file scope policy model and enforcement
tasks.desc: Define the file scope policy data model and enforcement from §9.4, including read/write roots, deny roots, and precedence rules.
tasks.goal: Control which files workers can read and write to prevent unauthorized modifications.
in_scope: Precedence rules: deny > write > read > outside
Post-run diff validation (check modified files against write_roots)
criteria: Path access checks follow precedence rules exactly
Post-run diff validation catches out-of-scope writes
Default policy is reasonable for V1
tasks.validation: Tests with various path/policy combinations
tasks.risks: Glob matching edge cases. Test with tricky paths.

tasks.title: Implement validation policy with profile selection
tasks.desc: Implement validation policy and the profile selection algorithm from §9.5.
tasks.goal: Determine which validation checks are required at each stage transition.
in_scope: Profile selection algorithm (task override > workflow template > task type > system default)
tasks.validation: Tests for profile selection with various input combinations
tasks.risks: Profile selection precedence must be exact. Test all paths.

tasks.title: Implement retry and escalation policy evaluation
tasks.desc: Implement retry policy and escalation policy evaluation from §9.6 and §9.7. Determine whether a failed task should retry, escalate, or fail permanently.
tasks.goal: Automate failure recovery decisions based on configurable thresholds.
in_scope: EscalationPolicy type with all trigger cases from §9.7.2
shouldRetry(task, policy) -> boolean with backoff calculation
criteria: Backoff calculated with exponential formula and cap
tasks.validation: Tests for various retry and escalation scenarios
tasks.risks: Escalation triggers are numerous. Ensure all are covered.

tasks.title: Implement hierarchical configuration resolution
tasks.desc: Implement the 8-layer hierarchical configuration resolution from §9.12: system defaults through operator emergency override.
tasks.goal: Enable flexible configuration where any level can override lower-level defaults.
in_scope: 8-layer precedence: system > environment > org > repo workflow > pool > task-type > task > operator override
Each resolved value records which layer supplied it
Config loading from code defaults and DB
criteria: Source tracking records which layer provided each value
tasks.validation: Tests with various override combinations
tasks.risks: Deep merge of nested config objects must handle arrays and nulls correctly.

tasks.title: Implement effective policy snapshot generation
tasks.desc: Implement the generation and persistence of effective policy snapshots that capture the resolved configuration for each worker run.
tasks.goal: Ensure every worker run has an immutable, reproducible policy snapshot.
in_scope: Snapshot includes: command_policy, file_scope_policy, validation_policy, retry_policy, escalation_policy, lease_policy, review_policy, retention_policy
Snapshot is immutable for life of run
tasks.validation: Test snapshot generation with various config overrides
tasks.risks: Snapshot must capture the EXACT config used. Any mismatch breaks reproducibility.

tasks.title: Implement validation runner abstraction
tasks.desc: Create the validation runner abstraction that manages validation profiles, selects checks, and orchestrates their execution.
tasks.goal: Provide a single entry point for running validation checks against any validation profile.
criteria: Validation runs all checks in profile
Required check failure makes overall result fail
Optional check failure doesn't fail overall
Skipped required checks handled per policy
tasks.validation: Tests with mock check executors
tasks.risks: Check execution order may matter for some repos. Execute sequentially for V1.

tasks.title: Implement test/lint/build command execution
tasks.desc: Implement the actual command execution for validation checks (test, lint, build, typecheck, security scan) within a workspace, using the policy-aware command wrapper.
tasks.goal: Execute validation commands and capture their results in a structured format.
in_scope: Execute validation commands via command wrapper
out_scope: Result interpretation (pass/fail is by exit code)
criteria: Commands execute in correct workspace directory
Exit code correctly maps to pass/fail
tasks.validation: Tests with real commands (echo, false, sleep) in temp directories
tasks.risks: Command execution is inherently risky. Rely on command wrapper for safety.

tasks.title: Implement ValidationResultPacket emission
tasks.desc: After validation checks complete, emit a schema-valid ValidationResultPacket and persist it as an artifact.
tasks.goal: Produce machine-readable validation results for the orchestrator and audit trail.
tasks.validation: End-to-end test: run validation, verify emitted packet matches schema
tasks.risks: None significant.

tasks.title: Implement validation gate checking for state transitions
tasks.desc: Integrate validation gates into the state transition service. Block transitions when required validation checks have not passed.
tasks.goal: Enforce quality gates at every stage transition that requires validation.
in_scope: IN_DEVELOPMENT → DEV_COMPLETE requires default-dev profile checks pass
POST_MERGE_VALIDATION → DONE requires merge-gate checks pass
Failed required validations block transition with validation_result_packet
out_scope: APPROVED → QUEUED_FOR_MERGE does NOT re-validate (per spec)
criteria: Gated transitions blocked without passing validation
Correct profile required at each gate
Clear error when validation missing or failed
tasks.validation: Tests for each gated transition with passing and failing validations
tasks.risks: Must not accidentally block non-gated transitions.

tasks.title: Implement Review Router with deterministic rules
tasks.desc: Build the Review Router that determines which specialist reviewers should review a task based on changed files, tags, risk level, and repository settings.
tasks.goal: Automatically route reviews to the right specialists based on deterministic rules.
in_scope: Rule evaluation in deterministic order from §10.6.2
Path-based rules, tag/domain rules, risk-based rules
Review routing rule configuration (JSON structure from §10.6.3)
criteria: Required and optional reviewers correctly determined
tasks.validation: Tests with diverse rule configurations and file patterns
tasks.risks: Rule evaluation order matters. Must follow §10.6.2 exactly.

tasks.title: Implement specialist reviewer job dispatch
tasks.desc: After review routing, create reviewer_dispatch jobs for each specialist reviewer, all sharing the same job_group_id for coordination.
tasks.goal: Fan out review work to specialist reviewers in parallel via the job queue.
in_scope: Create one reviewer_dispatch job per specialist
criteria: One job per specialist reviewer with shared group ID
Job payloads include reviewer-specific task packets
tasks.validation: Integration test: complete dev, verify review jobs created
tasks.risks: Review fan-out creates multiple jobs atomically. Use a transaction.

tasks.title: Implement lead reviewer dispatch with dependencies
tasks.desc: Create the lead_review_consolidation job that depends on all specialist reviewer jobs, so it only executes after all specialists have completed.
tasks.goal: Ensure lead reviewer sees all specialist reviews before making a decision.
in_scope: Create lead_review_consolidation job with depends_on_job_ids listing all reviewer jobs
Job only claimable when all dependencies are terminal
Lead reviewer TaskPacket includes all specialist review packets
criteria: Lead review job created with correct dependencies
Job not claimable until all specialist jobs complete
Lead reviewer receives all specialist review packets
tasks.validation: Integration test: complete all specialist reviews, verify lead job becomes claimable
tasks.risks: If a specialist job fails, lead review still triggers (reviews partial results).

tasks.title: Implement review decision application
tasks.desc: Process the LeadReviewDecisionPacket and apply the decision: approved, approved_with_follow_up, changes_requested, or escalated.
tasks.goal: Translate lead reviewer decisions into task state transitions and follow-up actions.
criteria: All four decision types handled correctly
Escalation triggered when review round limit exceeded
tasks.validation: Tests for each decision type including escalation trigger
tasks.risks: Review round limit check must use escalation policy. Don't hard-code.

tasks.title: Implement rework loop with rejection context
tasks.desc: Implement the CHANGES_REQUESTED → ASSIGNED rework loop. When rework is scheduled, the new TaskPacket includes rejection context with blocking issues and lead decision summary.
tasks.goal: Give reworking developers clear context about what needs to change.
in_scope: Build RejectionContext from lead review decision and blocking issues
Task re-enters scheduling flow (CHANGES_REQUESTED → ASSIGNED via scheduler)
criteria: RejectionContext correctly assembled from review decision
Blocking issues preserved from specialist and lead reviews
tasks.validation: End-to-end test: reject task, verify rework packet includes rejection context
tasks.risks: Must not lose information between review rounds. Preserve full rejection context.

tasks.title: Implement merge queue with ordering contract
tasks.desc: Implement the merge queue that accepts approved tasks, maintains ordering per §10.10, and dispatches merge work.
tasks.goal: Serialize merge operations to prevent integration conflicts.
in_scope: Queue ordering: priority > enqueue time > item ID
dequeueNext(repositoryId) — get next item for processing
criteria: Atomic claim prevents duplicate merge processing
tasks.validation: Tests with multiple items and priority ordering
tasks.risks: Queue ordering must be deterministic. Test tie-breaking carefully.

tasks.title: Implement rebase-and-merge execution
tasks.desc: Implement the rebase-and-merge strategy: rebase the task branch on latest main, run merge-gate validation, and merge if successful.
tasks.goal: Execute the default merge strategy reliably and capture results.
in_scope: Handle rebase conflicts (delegate to classifier T066)
criteria: Rebase executes on latest target branch
tasks.validation: Integration test with clean and conflicting merges
tasks.risks: Git operations can fail in unexpected ways. Handle all error cases.

tasks.title: Implement squash and merge-commit strategies
tasks.desc: Add squash and merge-commit strategies alongside the default rebase-and-merge.
tasks.goal: Support all three merge strategies specified in the architecture.
in_scope: Squash merge: squash all commits into one
tasks.validation: Tests for each merge strategy
tasks.risks: Squash loses individual commit history. Ensure this is acceptable per policy.

tasks.title: Implement merge conflict classification
tasks.desc: Implement the conflict classification logic from §10.10.2 that determines whether a merge conflict is reworkable or non-reworkable.
tasks.goal: Automatically classify merge failures to determine the correct recovery action.
in_scope: Classification: reworkable (fewer than max files, no protected paths) vs non-reworkable
tasks.validation: Tests with various conflict scenarios
tasks.risks: Protected path matching must use correct glob/prefix patterns.

tasks.title: Implement post-merge validation and failure policy
tasks.desc: Implement post-merge validation triggering and the severity-based failure policy from §9.11 for handling post-merge failures.
tasks.goal: Detect and respond to regressions after merge.
in_scope: Trigger merge-gate validation after successful merge
Response by severity: revert task, analysis agent, diagnostic task
Merge queue pause on critical failures
criteria: Revert tasks generated with correct scope
tasks.validation: Tests for each severity level and response
tasks.risks: Post-merge failures are high-impact. Response must be correct and timely.

tasks.title: Implement follow-up task generation
tasks.desc: Implement creation of follow-up tasks from approved_with_follow_up decisions, post-merge revert tasks, and diagnostic tasks.
tasks.goal: Automatically create follow-up work items so nothing is lost.
in_scope: Follow-up from approved_with_follow_up: create tasks from follow_up_task_refs
Hotfix tasks from analysis agent recommendations
Auto-set dependencies: follow-up depends on original task
criteria: Follow-up tasks created with correct metadata
Dependencies link follow-ups to source tasks
Tasks enter BACKLOG state for scheduling
tasks.validation: Test follow-up creation from various sources
tasks.risks: Follow-up task descriptions must be actionable. Include sufficient context.

tasks.title: Implement filesystem artifact storage
tasks.desc: Implement the filesystem-based artifact storage service with the structured directory layout from §7.11.
tasks.goal: Provide reliable artifact persistence that can be upgraded to object storage later.
in_scope: Subdirectories: packets/, runs/{runId}/(logs|outputs|validation), reviews/{reviewCycleId}/, merges/, summaries/
criteria: Artifacts stored in correct directory structure
Writes are atomic (no partial files on crash)
tasks.validation: Store and retrieve various artifact types
tasks.risks: Filesystem operations are not transactional with DB. Accept eventual consistency.

tasks.title: Implement artifact reference resolution and retrieval
tasks.desc: Implement artifact retrieval by reference path and by entity query (all artifacts for a task, run, review cycle, etc.).
tasks.goal: Make artifacts easily retrievable for the UI, API, and internal services.
in_scope: JSON artifact deserialization with schema version handling
tasks.validation: Store artifacts, then retrieve by various methods
tasks.risks: Directory traversal security — ensure paths stay within artifact root.

tasks.title: Implement summarization packet generation for retries
tasks.desc: Generate summarization packets that condense failed-run artifacts into bounded context for the next retry attempt.
tasks.goal: Give retry workers useful context about prior failures without overwhelming them with full logs.
in_scope: Summarize failed run: what was attempted, what failed, partial output
Reference full artifacts for detailed inspection
criteria: Summary stored as artifact and referenced correctly
tasks.validation: Generate summary from a simulated failed run
tasks.risks: Summaries must be useful but not too verbose. Balance is key.

tasks.title: Implement partial work snapshot on lease reclaim
tasks.desc: When a lease is reclaimed, capture the workspace state as partial artifacts for crash recovery context.
tasks.goal: Preserve whatever work was done before a crash for potential use in retries.
criteria: Partial artifacts captured when workspace is available
tasks.validation: Test with workspace in various states (clean, dirty, missing)
tasks.risks: Workspace may be in any state after a crash. Be maximally defensive.

tasks.title: Implement audit event recording on state transitions
tasks.desc: Ensure every state transition creates an AuditEvent record atomically within the same transaction. Record entity type, old/new state, actor, and metadata.
tasks.goal: Maintain a complete, tamper-evident audit trail of all state changes.
in_scope: AuditEvent creation in TransitionService (already sketched in T018)
All required fields: entity_type, entity_id, event_type, actor_type, actor_id, old_state, new_state
Metadata capturing transition context (trigger reason, policy applied, etc.)
Actor types: system, scheduler, worker, operator, policy
criteria: Every state transition has an audit event
Audit events have correct actor and metadata
No transition can occur without an audit event
tasks.validation: Run full lifecycle test, verify audit trail is complete
tasks.risks: Must not miss any transition. Ensure new transitions added later also create audits.

tasks.title: Implement audit event query service
tasks.desc: Build the query service for audit events supporting filtering by entity, time range, actor, and event type.
tasks.goal: Enable operators to reconstruct what happened for any task or entity.
in_scope: Query by entity_type + entity_id (timeline for a task)
criteria: Entity timeline returns all events in order
Pagination works for large result sets
Performance acceptable for typical query patterns
tasks.validation: Seed audit events and test various query patterns
tasks.risks: Large audit tables may slow queries. Ensure indexes are used.

tasks.title: Implement structured logging with correlation IDs
tasks.desc: Set up structured JSON logging with correlation fields (taskId, runId, workerId, reviewCycleId, etc.) throughout the application.
tasks.goal: Enable log-based debugging and tracing across all system components.
tasks.validation: Run application and verify log output format
tasks.risks: Logging must not impact performance. Use async logging.

tasks.title: Initialize OpenTelemetry TracerProvider
tasks.desc: Set up the OpenTelemetry SDK with TracerProvider, span exporters, and context propagation for the control plane service.
tasks.goal: Establish the tracing foundation for observability across orchestration steps.
tasks.validation: Start app, make API call, verify trace output
tasks.risks: OpenTelemetry SDK adds startup overhead. Keep it minimal for V1.

tasks.title: Instrument core orchestration paths with spans
tasks.desc: Add OpenTelemetry spans for the starter span tree from §10.13.2: task.assign, task.transition, worker.prepare, worker.run, validation.run, review.route, review.lead_decision, merge.prepare, merge.execute.
tasks.goal: Enable operators to trace a task's journey through all orchestration steps.
criteria: All starter spans created at correct points
tasks.validation: Run a task end-to-end and verify span tree matches §10.13.5
tasks.risks: Too many spans impacts performance. Stick to the starter set.

tasks.title: Implement Prometheus metrics endpoint
tasks.desc: Create a /metrics endpoint that exposes Prometheus-compatible metrics.
tasks.goal: Enable metrics scraping for monitoring and alerting.
in_scope: GET /metrics endpoint returning Prometheus text format
tasks.validation: curl http://localhost:3000/metrics and verify format
tasks.risks: Metric cardinality must stay low. Follow label rules from §10.13.4.

tasks.title: Implement starter metrics inventory
tasks.desc: Implement the starter metrics from §10.13.3: task transitions, worker runs, heartbeat timeouts, review cycles, merge attempts, validation runs, queue depth.
tasks.goal: Provide operators with key operational metrics from day one.
in_scope: Metric emission at correct instrumentation points
tasks.validation: Run operations and verify metrics increment
tasks.risks: Adding too many labels creates cardinality explosion. Be strict about §10.13.4.

tasks.title: Implement NestJS application bootstrap and module structure
tasks.desc: Bootstrap the NestJS application in apps/control-plane with module structure matching the domain modules, global error handling, request validation, and OpenAPI documentation.
tasks.goal: Establish the API framework so endpoint implementation can proceed in parallel.
in_scope: Request validation with class-validator or Zod
criteria: Global error handling returns structured errors
tasks.validation: Start app and test health endpoint
tasks.risks: NestJS module wiring can be complex. Start with minimal modules and expand.

tasks.title: Implement Project and Repository CRUD endpoints
tasks.desc: Create REST endpoints for Project and Repository CRUD operations.
tasks.goal: Enable project and repository management through the API.
criteria: All CRUD operations work for Projects and Repositories
tasks.validation: API tests for all endpoints
tasks.risks: API design should be stable. Review endpoint structure before implementing.

tasks.title: Implement Task management endpoints
tasks.desc: Create REST endpoints for Task CRUD, status queries, and filtering.
tasks.goal: Enable task management and visibility through the API.
in_scope: Filtering: by status, repository, priority, task_type
Include dependency information in detail response
criteria: Filtering works for all supported fields
New tasks start in BACKLOG state
tasks.validation: API tests for task operations and filtering
tasks.risks: Task query performance may need optimization for large datasets. Add indexes early.

tasks.title: Implement WorkerPool and AgentProfile endpoints
tasks.desc: Create REST endpoints for WorkerPool and AgentProfile management.
tasks.goal: Enable pool configuration through the API.
criteria: Pool status includes active worker count
tasks.validation: API tests for pool and profile management
tasks.risks: Pool configuration changes should not affect in-progress work.

tasks.title: Implement Artifact and Review packet retrieval endpoints
tasks.desc: Create REST endpoints for retrieving artifacts, packets, and review decisions.
tasks.goal: Enable the UI to display task artifacts, review details, and merge results.
criteria: Review history includes all cycles and decisions
tasks.validation: Seed data and test retrieval endpoints
tasks.risks: Large artifacts may need streaming. For V1, full response is acceptable.

tasks.title: Implement Audit, Policy, and Config endpoints
tasks.desc: Create REST endpoints for audit event queries, policy management, and configuration inspection.
tasks.goal: Expose audit trail, policy configuration, and system config through the API.
criteria: Audit queries work with all filter combinations
Effective config resolves correctly for given context
tasks.validation: API tests for audit, policy, and config operations
tasks.risks: Audit queries on large datasets need pagination. Ensure it's included.

tasks.title: Implement WebSocket gateway for live events
tasks.desc: Create a WebSocket gateway using NestJS @WebSocketGateway for real-time event delivery to UI clients.
tasks.goal: Enable live UI updates without polling.
in_scope: Connection auth (simple token for V1 local mode)
tasks.validation: Connect with a WebSocket client and verify event delivery
tasks.risks: WebSocket scaling for V1 local mode is fine. Don't over-engineer.

tasks.title: Implement task state change event broadcasting
tasks.desc: Broadcast task state change events via WebSocket when the transition service commits transitions.
tasks.goal: Keep the UI in sync with task state changes in real time.
in_scope: Subscribe to transition service domain events
Broadcast task state changes to /tasks namespace
Event payload: taskId, oldState, newState, timestamp, actor
criteria: State changes broadcast within milliseconds of commit
Room-based targeting works for task-specific subscriptions
tasks.validation: Change task state and verify WebSocket event received
tasks.risks: Event emission must be after commit, not before. Don't emit on rollback.

tasks.title: Implement queue and worker status broadcasting
tasks.desc: Broadcast worker pool status updates and merge queue changes via WebSocket.
tasks.goal: Keep the UI pool and queue views updated in real time.
tasks.validation: Monitor WebSocket events during task execution
tasks.risks: Too many events can overwhelm the UI. Throttle appropriately.

tasks.title: Initialize React SPA with Vite, Tailwind, shadcn/ui
tasks.desc: Bootstrap the React SPA in apps/web-ui with Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui component library.
tasks.goal: Establish the frontend application foundation.
in_scope: Basic component primitives (Button, Card, Table, etc.)
criteria: App builds and runs via pnpm dev
tasks.validation: pnpm dev in web-ui and verify app loads in browser
tasks.risks: shadcn/ui requires specific Tailwind config. Follow their setup guide exactly.

tasks.title: Implement API client layer with TanStack Query
tasks.desc: Create the API client layer using TanStack Query for data fetching, caching, and mutation with typed API functions.
tasks.goal: Provide a robust data fetching layer for all UI views.
in_scope: Query hooks for all major entities (tasks, projects, pools, etc.)
criteria: TanStack Query provider wraps the app
All major entity queries have hooks
tasks.validation: Verify queries fetch data from running backend
tasks.risks: API types must match backend DTOs. Consider generating types from OpenAPI.

tasks.title: Implement WebSocket client for live updates
tasks.desc: Create a WebSocket client that connects to the backend event gateway and invalidates TanStack Query caches on real-time events.
tasks.goal: Make the UI reactive to backend state changes without manual refresh.
in_scope: TanStack Query cache invalidation on events
tasks.validation: Change task state via API, verify UI updates automatically
tasks.risks: WebSocket reconnection must be robust. Use exponential backoff.

tasks.title: Build app shell with navigation layout
tasks.desc: Build the application shell with sidebar navigation, breadcrumbs, and content area layout.
tasks.goal: Provide consistent navigation and layout for all feature views.
in_scope: Sidebar with navigation links to all primary views
Views: Dashboard, Tasks, Pools, Reviews, Merge Queue, Config, Audit
criteria: Responsive layout functions on various screen sizes
tasks.validation: Navigate to all routes and verify layout
tasks.risks: Layout decisions affect all views. Get review before implementing features.

tasks.title: Build dashboard view with system health summary
tasks.desc: Build the dashboard view showing system health: task state summary, queue depths, worker pool status, and recent activity.
tasks.goal: Give operators an at-a-glance view of system status.
criteria: Dashboard shows task counts by state
Recent activity updates in real time
tasks.validation: View dashboard with real data from backend
tasks.risks: Dashboard queries may be slow. Use appropriate aggregation endpoints.

tasks.title: Build task board with status filtering and pagination
tasks.desc: Build the task board view with table display, status filtering, priority sorting, and pagination.
tasks.goal: Enable operators to browse and manage all tasks.
in_scope: Task table with columns: ID, title, status, priority, repository, updated_at
out_scope: Task creation form (can use API directly)
criteria: Filtering works for all supported fields
tasks.validation: View task board with various filter combinations
tasks.risks: Large task lists need pagination. Ensure backend supports it.

tasks.title: Build task detail timeline view
tasks.desc: Build the task detail view showing complete task information, audit timeline, packets, and artifacts.
tasks.goal: Enable operators to inspect any task and reconstruct what happened.
in_scope: Packet display (task packet, dev result, review decisions)
tasks.validation: View task detail for a task with full lifecycle data
tasks.risks: JSON viewer for large packets may be slow. Use lazy rendering.

tasks.title: Build worker pool monitoring panel
tasks.desc: Build the worker pool monitoring view showing pool status, active workers, and configuration.
tasks.goal: Give operators visibility into worker pool health and utilization.
in_scope: Pool list with status (enabled/disabled), active workers, max concurrency
Pool detail: configuration, active workers, recent runs
criteria: All pools displayed with correct status
Pool detail shows configuration and workers
tasks.validation: View pools page with active workers
tasks.risks: Worker status must update in real time. Ensure WebSocket events work.

tasks.title: Build review center view
tasks.desc: Build the review center showing tasks in review, review decisions, blocking issues, and review cycle history.
tasks.goal: Enable operators to monitor review quality and identify bottlenecks.
in_scope: Tasks in IN_REVIEW and CHANGES_REQUESTED states
Specialist review packets with issue lists
Review round count and escalation warnings
tasks.validation: View review center with tasks in various review states
tasks.risks: Review data is complex. Focus on clarity over completeness initially.

tasks.title: Build merge queue view
tasks.desc: Build the merge queue view showing queued items, their position, and merge execution status.
tasks.goal: Enable operators to monitor the merge pipeline.
in_scope: Merge queue table: position, task, status, enqueued time
Queue pause indicator for critical failures
tasks.validation: View merge queue during active merge processing
tasks.risks: Queue updates must be real-time. Ensure WebSocket events propagate.

tasks.title: Build configuration editor view
tasks.desc: Build the configuration editor for viewing and modifying pool configurations, policy sets, prompt templates, and routing rules.
tasks.goal: Enable operators to tune system behavior through the UI.
in_scope: JSON editor for complex config objects
criteria: Configuration viewable for all config types
tasks.validation: Edit a config value and verify it takes effect
tasks.risks: Config editing can break the system. Add validation and confirmation.

tasks.title: Build audit explorer view
tasks.desc: Build the audit explorer for searching and browsing audit events across the system.
tasks.goal: Enable operators to investigate and reconstruct system behavior.
in_scope: Audit event search with filters (entity, time range, actor, event type)
Quick link from task detail to task-specific audit trail
tasks.validation: Search audit events and verify results
tasks.risks: Large result sets need pagination. Ensure it's included.

tasks.title: Implement operator action API endpoints
tasks.desc: Create API endpoints for all operator actions from §6.2: pause/resume, requeue, force unblock, change priority, reassign pool, rerun reviewer, override merge order, reopen task.
tasks.goal: Give operators programmatic control over the automated workflow.
criteria: All operator actions from §6.2 have endpoints
tasks.validation: Test each operator action via API
tasks.risks: Operator actions bypass normal flow. Must still respect state machine invariants.

tasks.title: Implement state transition guards for manual actions
tasks.desc: Add safety guards for operator actions that ensure manual overrides respect state machine invariants and system safety constraints.
tasks.goal: Prevent operators from putting the system in an inconsistent state.
in_scope: Validate that force_unblock has a reason
Validate reopen doesn't violate active lease
Validate merge order override doesn't skip required validations
Validate cancel doesn't lose in-progress work without confirmation
criteria: Descriptive error messages for guard failures
Sensitive actions logged with elevated audit
tasks.validation: Test guard enforcement for each operator action
tasks.risks: Guards must balance safety with operator flexibility. Don't be too restrictive.

tasks.title: Implement escalation resolution flow
tasks.desc: Implement the escalation resolution flow: operators can retry, cancel, or mark as externally completed for ESCALATED tasks.
tasks.goal: Provide a clear path for operators to resolve tasks that require human judgment.
in_scope: Resolution types: retry (→ ASSIGNED), cancel (→ CANCELLED), mark_done (→ DONE)
Retry: clear escalation reason, create new scheduling job
Cancel: preserve escalation context in audit
Mark done: require reason/evidence for external completion
criteria: All three resolution types work correctly
tasks.validation: Test each escalation resolution path
tasks.risks: mark_done bypasses normal quality checks. Require explicit reason.

tasks.title: Integrate operator controls into task detail UI
tasks.desc: Add operator action controls to the task detail view: state-dependent action buttons, confirmation dialogs, and result feedback.
tasks.goal: Enable operators to take actions directly from the task inspection view.
in_scope: Context-sensitive action buttons (only show valid actions for current state)
Confirmation dialogs with reason text input for destructive actions
criteria: Only valid actions shown per state
tasks.validation: Test operator actions from task detail view
tasks.risks: State changes may be concurrent with UI. Handle optimistic concurrency gracefully.

tasks.title: Integrate operator controls into pool and merge queue UI
tasks.desc: Add operator controls to pool management and merge queue views.
tasks.goal: Enable operators to manage pools and merge queue from the UI.
in_scope: Pool: move task to different pool
tasks.validation: Test operator actions in pool and merge queue views
tasks.risks: Pool changes affect scheduling. Show impact warnings.

tasks.title: Create test harness with fake runner and workspace
tasks.desc: Build the integration test harness with fake runner adapter, fake workspace manager, fake clock, and test database setup/teardown.
tasks.goal: Enable reliable, deterministic integration testing of the full system.
in_scope: Test database setup (in-memory SQLite with migrations)
Test fixture factories for all entities
Test helper: runTaskToState(targetState) for common test setup
criteria: runTaskToState helper works for all reachable states
tasks.validation: Use test harness in a sample integration test
tasks.risks: Test doubles must accurately simulate real behavior. Validate against specs.

tasks.title: Integration test: full task lifecycle BACKLOG to DONE
tasks.desc: Build an integration test that drives a task through the complete happy path: BACKLOG → READY → ASSIGNED → IN_DEVELOPMENT → DEV_COMPLETE → IN_REVIEW → APPROVED → QUEUED_FOR_MERGE → MERGING → POST_MERGE_VALIDATION → DONE.
tasks.goal: Verify that the entire orchestration pipeline works end-to-end. This corresponds to V1 Milestone 1.
in_scope: Worker executes and submits valid DevResultPacket
criteria: Task completes full lifecycle to DONE
Every state transition produces an audit event
No duplicate assignments or race conditions
