tasks.validation: Run integration test and verify all assertions
tasks.risks: This is the most complex test. Debug failures carefully.

tasks.title: Integration test: review rejection and rework loop
tasks.desc: Test the review rejection path: developer submits, reviewer rejects with blocking issues, task re-enters ASSIGNED with rejection context, developer fixes, reviewer approves.
tasks.goal: Verify the rework loop works correctly with rejection context propagation.
in_scope: First dev attempt: submit valid but flawed code
Rework: verify rejection context in new TaskPacket
criteria: Rework TaskPacket includes rejection context with blocking issues
tasks.validation: Run integration test
tasks.risks: Rejection context must be complete and accurate.

tasks.title: Integration test: merge conflict and failure paths
tasks.desc: Test merge failure scenarios: reworkable conflict (→ CHANGES_REQUESTED), non-reworkable conflict (→ FAILED), and post-merge validation failure.
tasks.goal: Verify merge failure handling per the conflict classification and post-merge failure policies.
in_scope: Reworkable conflict: few files, no protected paths → CHANGES_REQUESTED
Non-reworkable conflict: many files or protected paths → FAILED
Post-merge validation failure: severity classification and response
Revert task generation for critical failures
criteria: State transitions correct for each scenario
Revert tasks created for critical failures
tasks.validation: Run integration tests for each merge failure scenario
tasks.risks: Merge failure simulation must be realistic enough to exercise classification logic.

tasks.title: Integration test: lease timeout and crash recovery
tasks.desc: Test worker failure scenarios: heartbeat timeout, process crash, and graceful completion edge cases.
tasks.goal: Verify that lease management correctly recovers from worker failures.
in_scope: Heartbeat timeout: worker stops sending heartbeats → lease reclaim → retry
Process crash: worker exits non-zero → lease status CRASHED → retry or escalate
Grace period: result arrives just after timeout → accepted
Retry exhaustion: max retries exceeded → ESCALATED
tasks.validation: Run integration tests
tasks.risks: Time-based tests are inherently tricky. Ensure FakeClock is used consistently.

tasks.title: Integration test: escalation triggers and resolution
tasks.desc: Test escalation trigger scenarios and operator resolution paths.
tasks.goal: Verify that escalation works correctly for all trigger conditions and resolution types.
in_scope: Trigger: max review rounds exceeded → ESCALATED
criteria: All tested trigger conditions produce ESCALATED
tasks.validation: Run integration tests for escalation scenarios
tasks.risks: Escalation is a safety mechanism. Must work reliably.

phases.title: Foundation
phases.goal: Establish the monorepo, toolchain, database, domain types, and core infrastructure so all subsequent work can proceed safely.
phases.why: Nothing can be built without a working monorepo, database, type system, and test framework. This phase has zero product functionality but enables everything.
exit_criteria: Monorepo builds, lints, and tests
All database tables created via migrations
All repositories pass CRUD tests
All packet schemas validate against spec examples
CI pipeline runs green
phases.risks: Stack decisions (NestJS, Drizzle, etc.) must be finalized before starting.

phases.title: Core Domain Skeleton
phases.goal: Implement the state machines, transition engine, job queue, scheduler, lease management, dependency engine, and policy system — the deterministic control plane core.
phases.why: The control plane core is the foundation of correctness. All orchestration, scheduling, and safety depends on these modules.
exit_criteria: State machine validates all transitions correctly
Job queue supports claim/complete/fail with dependencies
Scheduler assigns tasks to pools
Leases enforce exclusivity with heartbeat monitoring
Dependencies compute readiness correctly
All policies resolve and snapshot correctly
phases.risks: State machine complexity. Concurrency in lease management. Policy resolution precedence.

phases.title: First End-to-End Vertical Slice
phases.goal: Complete the worker runtime, workspace management, validation runner, review pipeline, merge pipeline, artifact service, and API layer to achieve a full task lifecycle from BACKLOG to DONE.
phases.why: This proves the system works end-to-end. It is the V1 proof of concept.
exit_criteria: A task can be created via API and driven through the full lifecycle to DONE
Worker executes in isolated workspace and submits valid packets
Review pipeline routes, reviews, and consolidates
Merge queue processes and merges correctly
All artifacts persisted and retrievable
Audit trail complete for every transition
phases.risks: Copilot CLI integration may require experimentation. Merge pipeline has complex failure modes.

phases.title: UI and Operability
phases.goal: Build the web UI, real-time events, and operator action system so humans can monitor and control the autonomous factory.
phases.why: Without a UI, the system is a black box. Operators need visibility and intervention capabilities.
exit_criteria: Dashboard shows live system state
Task board enables browsing and filtering
Task detail shows complete timeline and artifacts
Operator can take all manual actions from the UI
Real-time updates work for all major state changes
phases.risks: UI complexity is high. Prioritize task board and detail views over analytics.

phases.title: Hardening and Operational Readiness
phases.goal: Add observability, comprehensive integration tests, and operational hardening to prepare for real autonomous operation.
phases.why: The system must be observable, recoverable, and thoroughly tested before running autonomously.
exit_criteria: OpenTelemetry traces cover task lifecycle end-to-end
Prometheus metrics endpoint returns all starter metrics
Integration tests pass for all critical paths
Fault injection tests verify recovery
V1 milestones from §3.6 are all met
phases.risks: Integration tests are complex and potentially brittle. Observability adds overhead.
